objective: Améliorer MissionPulse sur tous les fronts: ESLint, scoring sémantique, tests E2E, documentation, connecteurs, performance, et fonctionnalités UX

description: ESLint v9 flat config with TypeScript, Svelte 5, Chrome globals
TypeScript config for ESLint type-checked rules
Added typescript-eslint, globals, @eslint/js dependencies; updated lint scripts
Replaced by eslint.config.js

reason: Blocks svelte/store (forces runes) and core importing shell (FC&IS)

description: Added diagnostics interface and metrics tracking
Bug fix: uses bestScore (semanticScore ?? score) for notifications
Immutable semantic enrichment with diagnostics logging
Prefers semanticScore for display and color coding
Added 2 tests for semantic score preference

content: Semantic scoring fully integrated in scan flow
reason: All components now use semanticScore when available, with graceful fallback

description: Comprehensive 418-line README with all sections
10 E2E tests for favorites flow
10 E2E tests for hidden missions flow
16 E2E tests for settings flow
10 E2E tests for export flow (JSON/CSV/Markdown)
12 E2E tests for scan lifecycle flow

reason: 58 new tests added (126 total vs 68 before)

description: Added validateParserOutput and validateMissionFields
Added startDate validation and runner tests
Graceful handling of non-JSON responses
Added validateParserOutput + validateMissionFields checks
Added parallel mode with progress logging

content: Health checks now use parser validation
reason: Ensures parser contracts are validated against real HTML/API responses

description: Added lazy loading attributes for favicons
Added CSS content-visibility for zero-JS virtualization
Reduced debounce from 300ms to 150ms
Optimized filtering with pre-computed search index
Replaced chained filters with single for-loop

content: Used browser-native lazy loading instead of IntersectionObserver
reason: Only 3-5 small favicons - IO overhead exceeds benefit

description: Pure rate-limiting logic for notifications
7 tests for notification rate limiting
Enhanced with threshold, rate limiting, grouping
TJM history types (TJMTrend, TJMRecord, TJMStats)
Pure TJM history functions (extractRecords, getStatsForStack, etc.)
Shell storage for TJM history via chrome.storage.local
Added TJM recording after mission persistence
Added TrendBadge for TJM trend indication
34 tests for TJM history pure functions

reason: Filters noise while showing meaningful trends

description: Core imports Shell: line 8 imports AppSettings from '../../shell/storage/chrome-storage'. Should import from core/types/ instead (AppSettingsFromSchema exists in core/types/schemas.ts)
Test helper makeMission() missing 'startDate' field from Mission interface. Works at runtime due to Partial<Mission> spread but is structurally incomplete.

content: All 7 target files (notification-filter, notification-rate-limit, tjm.ts, tjm-history/index.ts, scanner.ts, notify-missions.ts, tjm-history.ts) pass FC&IS validation. Core files have zero async, zero I/O, zero Date.now(), zero console.log, zero chrome.*. Shell files correctly import from Core and delegate pure computation. One pre-existing violation found in core/backup/backup.ts (not in target files).

description: FC&IS violation: core backup module imported AppSettings from shell storage
resolution: Extracted AppSettings to src/lib/core/types/app-settings.ts and re-exported it from shell storage

content: Normalized feed labels and ARIA to preserve existing Playwright selector contract
reason: Worker UI changes drifted from established test selectors (Rafraichir, Voir favoris, Voir toutes, Rechercher..., Missions)

description: Removed unused cached-path local variable

content: Normalized comprehensive-improvements documentation to 58 new E2E tests
reason: Per-file worker artifacts total 58 tests and supersede earlier inconsistent summary text

issues.description: makeMission() helper missing 'startDate' field from Mission interface. Works due to Partial<Mission> spread but is structurally incomplete.
Minor ESLint curly-brace style violations (lines 34, 38). Pre-existing config expectation not yet enforced codebase-wide.
